skill.name: analyzing-test-quality
skill.description: Analyzes test suites for quality, reliability, coverage gaps, maintainability problems, and common testing anti-patterns across frameworks.
skill.summary: Evaluate test suite quality and prioritize practical improvements.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the weak cryptography and Markdown backtick detections are false positives from documentation, templates, and example code. The confirmed risk is a bundled Bash script that recursively enumerates and reads local project files to calculate test metrics. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential access, network exfiltration, persistence, destructive behavior, or malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Recursive Local Test Metrics Script
medium_findings.description: The bundled Bash script runs find, grep, cat, wc, and bc against the current project to count tests, source files, and testing patterns. The commands use fixed arguments and do not show command injection or exfiltration, but they read broad local source and test file contents when executed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly executes local shell utilities and reads matched project files. Confidence is not higher because the behavior is aligned with the stated metrics feature and no malicious destination or user-controlled command construction is present.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: Many external command alerts in SKILL.md and the report template are fenced Markdown examples or placeholder report snippets. These are documentation content, not code that executes automatically when the skill is loaded.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged regions are visibly inside Markdown examples or templates. They are not connected to an execution path in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to match ordinary words and identifiers such as description text, describe blocks, and checklist headings. No hashing, encryption, credential handling, or cryptographic API usage was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines do not contain cryptographic operations. The matches are consistent with scanner keyword collisions on prose and variable names.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alerts Are Testing Guidance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings point to test pyramid and anti-pattern guidance, not host inspection or network discovery. The only operational scanning found is local project test-file enumeration in the metrics script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content describes testing concepts and report sections. I found no commands for host reconnaissance or network probing in those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tooling and Local File Enumeration
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill allows Bash and includes a shell script that enumerates files under the working directory. This is useful for metrics, but users should run it only in repositories they intend to analyze.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Bash capability and recursive file enumeration are explicit in the files. The pattern is potentially sensitive but does not include destructive or network behavior.

content.value_statement: Weak tests can hide defects, slow delivery, and create false confidence. This skill reviews test suites, metrics, and patterns to recommend focused improvements.
actual_capabilities: Reviews test suites for readability, reliability, maintainability, coverage quality, and execution speed.
Identifies common testing anti-patterns such as flaky assertions, shared state, over-mocking, and assertion roulette.
Provides a reusable checklist for scoring structure, coverage, reliability, maintainability, and performance.
Includes a report template for documenting findings, priority, impact, and recommended fixes.
Offers a Bash metrics script that counts test files, source files, test blocks, setup hooks, mocks, async tests, and skipped tests.
Explains mutation testing concepts, Stryker setup, coverage thresholds, and test pyramid balance.
limitations: It provides framework-agnostic guidance, not deep framework-specific recipes for Jest, Playwright, or other tools.
The metrics script focuses on JavaScript and TypeScript file patterns and may miss other languages.
Quality scores require human judgment because coverage and counts do not prove test effectiveness.
The bundled script reads local project files when executed and should be used only on intended repositories.

use_cases.title: Review a Test Suite Before Release
use_cases.description: Assess coverage gaps, flaky patterns, skipped tests, and maintainability risks before a production release.

use_cases.title: Improve Legacy Tests
use_cases.description: Find brittle structure, shared state, weak assertions, and missing edge cases in an inherited codebase.

use_cases.title: Create a Quality Baseline
use_cases.description: Use checklist scoring and metrics to define current test health and track future improvement work.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Test Quality Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small test suite.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the tests in this repository for readability, reliability, and maintainability. Summarize the three most important improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Coverage Gap Assessment
prompt_templates.scenario: Review coverage results and source risk.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my coverage report and test files. Identify high-risk untested behavior, weak assertions, and missing edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Flakiness and Isolation Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Find reliability risks in existing tests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these tests for flaky behavior, timing dependencies, shared state, order dependence, and cleanup problems. Prioritize fixes by impact.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Test Strategy Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a test quality improvement plan using coverage, mutation testing, test pyramid balance, maintainability, and CI reliability as inputs.

output_examples.input: Review a service test file with many setup helpers and repeated assertions.
output: Summary: Tests cover the happy path but hide important setup details.
Main risk: Shared mutable fixtures make failures hard to diagnose.
Recommendation: Use explicit factories and split unrelated assertions into focused tests.

output_examples.input: Analyze coverage results that show high line coverage but low branch coverage.
output: Summary: Current coverage may overstate confidence because decision paths are weakly tested.
Main risk: Error handling, boundary values, and alternate branches may break unnoticed.
Recommendation: Add scenario-based tests for each branch before increasing thresholds.

output_examples.input: Evaluate a suite with intermittent CI failures.
output: Summary: Reliability issues are likely caused by timing waits and incomplete cleanup.
Main risk: CI failures reduce trust in real regressions.
Recommendation: Replace fixed delays with condition waits and reset state after each test.

best_practices: Run analysis on a clear repository state so findings map to current files and tests.
Combine metrics with code review because high coverage does not prove strong assertions.
Prioritize flaky tests, skipped tests, and missing critical-path coverage before cosmetic cleanup.
anti_patterns: Treating coverage percentage as the only quality target.
Running the bundled metrics script in directories that contain unrelated or sensitive files.
Applying framework-agnostic advice without checking the conventions of the project test framework.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and includes an optional metrics script that a user or agent may run.

faq.question: Which languages does the metrics script support?
faq.answer: The script is designed for JavaScript and TypeScript file patterns.

faq.question: Can it replace framework-specific testing skills?
faq.answer: No. Use framework-specific skills when you need exact Jest, Playwright, Vitest, or Mocha patterns.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes Bash tooling that recursively reads local project files for metrics. No malicious behavior was found.

faq.question: Does it support mutation testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains mutation testing concepts, Stryker setup, interpretation, and CI integration.

faq.question: What should I provide for the best review?
faq.answer: Provide test files, source files, coverage results, CI failure details, and any known flaky test examples.
